text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development by ensuring that code works as expected. Unit tests check individual components in isolation, allowing developers to catch bugs early. Integration tests verify that different parts of the system work together smoothly. A popular approach is test-driven development (TDD), where developers write tests before writing the actual code. This process encourages thoughtful design and helps prevent errors from the start. Together, these testing practices build confidence in the codebase. When a project has thorough tests, developers can make changes or add features without fear of breaking existing functionality. Ultimately, good testing saves time and reduces stress.
